name: How should I prepare my home before the window installers arrive?
headline: How should I prepare my home before the window installers arrive?

mainEntity.name: How should I prepare my home before the window installers arrive?
mainEntity.text: How should I prepare my home before the window installers arrive?

acceptedAnswer.text: Before your window installation day in Pacifica, Daly City, San Bruno, or nearby cities, you should **clear space around each window** and protect your belongings. Move furniture at least a few feet away, take down blinds, curtains, and window treatments, and remove any wall decor that might be in the way. This helps the crew work quickly and safely. It’s also a good idea to cover nearby furniture and floors with sheets or plastic, since dust may fall when old windows and trim are removed. If you have an alarm system with window sensors, contact your provider about temporary removal or reattachment after the new windows are in. Homeowners often forget about outdoor access. Make sure gates aren’t locked, and move items like grills, potted plants, or bikes away from exterior walls where installers need to work. Another common pitfall is not planning for pets and kids. Loud noise, open doors, and workers coming in and out can be stressful and unsafe. Consider having pets in a separate room or out of the house and explain to children where they shouldn’t go during the project. Having everything ready before the crew arrives saves time and reduces the chance of accidental damage. It also makes the whole experience smoother and less stressful for you.
